messages.author: DFW_Nerd
messages.text: Does anyone know if any work has been done towards adding this hardware? I know the T5S3 without GPS is listed as a community supported item, but that firmware borks the GPS version. I'm not sure why, but it makes the display non-functional and the backlight blinks forever until it dies. My darling wife bought me two of these for Christmas, but didn't realize the difference on with/without GPS for firmware support and returning them to China is impractical.
messages.content_html: Does anyone know if any work has been done towards adding this hardware? messages.author: NL11673 / PA9900 / 19RF761
messages.content_html: https://buymeacoffee.com/ripplebiz/e/478441 Here it is.
embeds_html:
T5S3 Pro 'Ultra' firmware (ver 8.1)
v8.1 changes:* Kid Mode bug fix* Group channel chat: new 'Set Scope...' menu* Support for mapping Polish characters to simple Latin characters* Room messages rendering fix.* Radio presets updated.v8.0

messages.author: DFW_Nerd
messages.text: Thanks for prodding me on this. I found that Ripple 8.2 is now on the flasher website and it loads and runs properly. I'm still working on settings to it talks to my other nodes, but it gets GPS lock, sets time, and is generally operational.
messages.content_html: Thanks for prodding me on this. I found that Ripple 8.2 is now on the flasher website and it loads and runs properly. I'm still working on settings to it talks to my other nodes, but it gets GPS lock, sets time, and is generally operational.

messages.author: DFW_Nerd
messages.text: I don't know about using it as an e-reader but once I re-flashed 9.0 erasing it and took the default network name for the US, it was immediately able to beacon my other devices and all are talking now. This is pretty cool. I think @Scott is the right man for thanks on this one.
messages.content_html: I don't know about using it as an e-reader but once I re-flashed 9.0 erasing it and took the default network name for the US, it was immediately able to beacon my other devices and all are talking now. This is pretty cool. I think @Scott is the right man for thanks on this one.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Ceres Station 🌑
messages.text: hey @Scott one question; is there a way to turn GPS off completely?
messages.content_html: hey @Scott one question; is there a way to turn GPS off completely?

messages.author: Scott
messages.text: Not on the T5, unfortunately. LilyGo didn't provide a GPIO for that.
messages.content_html: Not on the T5, unfortunately. LilyGo didn't provide a GPIO for that.
